Blue Valley.cdMessage:
Good week last week. 

Emphasis this week- If patients brings up their family members or partners work on getting them into care!! This is a small habit that can grow your schedule exponentially!

Crossroads.cdMessage: We fell just short of our good goal but hit our new patient goals last week! Josh has a crazy week this week so let's try and support him as best we can. Q3 meeting will be on Wednesday morning, we'll cover our goals for the upcoming quarter.

KCK.cdMessage: Solid week last week! Hoping to get Madi busier soon, especially with her adding hours here starting next monday. Let's try and focus this week on getting back out in the community. Trying to set up some CE with old and new businesses or races. Fall is a big time for races as it is nicer weather.

Leawood.cdMessage: 490 - 26 last week. 502 is our weekly record and once broken, its pizza party time. Quarterly recap this Friday from 1:15-2PM in large conference room to review previous quarter and have outlook for Q3.

Lees Summit.cdMessage:
Good week with 341 visits. Great job Katie coming out the gate with a great 1st week!

I’ve also appreciate seeing everyone else’s visits consistently move up! Keep up the great work!

Let’s finalize a date for our team get together!

Liberty.cdMessage: Another solid week for us with a good amount of new patients! F.I.T. hit a new weekly record last week for total visits. We'll have a quarterly meeting on Wednesday at 1pm to review Q2 and discuss goals for Q3.

Olathe.cdMessage:
IF you see a NP access window that is not the correct color, please change it to the appropriate green color. This will allow PCCs to more easily see the access windows. You can do so by hitting info/edit, selecting "type" at the top, and selecting NP access block under the drop down. Please scan future weeks to complete. I also want to put it on you guys to add more NP access blocks to the months I had not gotten to. It was tedious to go through every provider and add blocks, so please complete on your own. I will audit schedules to make sure they are put on. I believe I have gotten through August right now. If you need help adding, let me know!

Continue to be diligent about cleaning up rooms and most importantly, the treatment space. As we have less and less space, picking up your KB, exercise mat, DB will be even more important. We do have overflow space over by the ice machine in the back corner of Homefield training area. Feel free to do as much exercises with patients out in the Homefield area, as we are short on space.

Contacts: Everyone needs to have provided 2 new contacts to Krista, Winnie, or Payton. I do not think I have done a good job following up on these. This involves getting an email, phone number to them so they can reach out about us doing events or offering other services, such as first aid. I will be following up with Krista to make sure our whole team is accounted for.

Overland Park.cdMessage:
Decent week last week, keep pushing the schedule out and reaffirming to patients the "why" when they are here for the initial appointments. This will help out with scheduling out, building a full schedule, and achieving our goals. 

Re-engagement Phase 2 should be going out soon, either this week or next, so we will be sure to hammer that out to get more patients back in.

Paola.cdMessage:
Better week overall, but not where we want to be. New patients have been down, aside of the end of last week. Scheduling out looks good overall, but the push to have people on the schedule, reduction of cancels, and getting new patients in the door. 
Inspirational Quote:
"If you stay ready, you do not have to get ready"

Shawnee.cdMessage:
SHAWNEE DOES IT AGAIN! Another record visit count week for 2026 at 393. 
Shoutout to Cam for seeing a whopping 67 patients last week!!
Shoutout to Adam for a scheduling out of 8.5 last week!
Our quarterly clinic meeting is next week, Monday, July 27th at 1PM. Bring your lunch and we will get everyone coffee.

It came to my attention last week that not everyone at Shawnee has an ambassador ! If you do not have one, you have until August 1st to tell me who you're targeting and trying to recruit to be your ambassador. If you need help, ask!

Thanks everyone !!

importantInfo.title: Check out SharePoint!
importantInfo.body: All of F.I.T.'s important documents, SOPs, and pertinent forms are now in Microsoft SharePoint. To get there, go to your email inbox, click the 9 dots in the top left corner of your screen, and then click "SharePoint". Take some time soon to familiarize yourself with the "F.I.T. Muscle & Joint Clinic" and "F.I.T. Community Engagement" folders, along with all of their subfolders. Use the search bar at the top to find what you need.

importantInfo.title: BOL: Vision and Values Survey
importantInfo.body: Please see the email in your inbox from Krista containing a survey that will ask you which F.I.T. team member reminds you of F.I.T.'s vision and values. Please answer honestly to who is passionate, unique, resilient, fun, has integrity, and maintains trusting relationships. Winners will receive the opportunity to go to a Chiefs game!

importantInfo.title: Pain Action Window Reminder
importantInfo.body: Everyone, please try your best to get new patients in fast. This minimizes the possibility of them going somewhere else or thinking they can fix their pain on their own. If your schedules are full, ask the patient if they feel comfortable getting their exam with a different provider or at another location even to start their care fast and then have the new patient schedule out on the provider's schedule that they requested.

importantInfo.title: Answering the Phone
importantInfo.body: Providers, your PCCs goal is to answer the phone 100% of the time that it rings. If you are at the front desk and you hear the phone ring, please step aside and let your PCC answer the phone.

importantInfo.title: Update: Maternity Leave Policy
importantInfo.body: Please see the most recent email from Bridgette regarding a change in the maternity leave policy.

importantInfo.title: New Scheduling Out Game!
importantInfo.body:
We are running another scheduling out game starting this week! For those of you who may be newer and are unaware, We will be tracking the number of visits that every one of your new patients schedules out At their first visit. Remember providers are to try their hardest to schedule out their new patients in their room. Only ask for PCC to schedule out your new patient if you are running behind. Here are the details:
1. Chiros & PTs/OTs are being tracked separately as PTs and OTs often have the advantage of a presecription for therapy from a referring provider.
2. The goal is to schedule out your new patients' 8+ visits.
3. START: June 29th // END: August 31st

PRIZES:
1st Place Chiro + 1st Place PT/OT gets tickets to a Chiefs game
2nd Place Chiro + 2nd Place PT gets $100
3rd Place Chiro + 3rd Place PT gets $50
Everyone gets $5 per NP scheduled out 8+ visits.

importantInfo.title: Pay at F.I.T. is Changing Frequency
importantInfo.body: Per Matt Lane's most recent monthly communication, everyone will now be getting paid on the 1st and 15th of every month starting 2027. This is to better predict expenses each quarter, as our previous pay frequency would have an extra pay period in some quarters, leaving the profit pot to take a hit from that.

importantInfo.title: Boulders and Boulder Leader Information
importantInfo.body:
Here are all of the following boulders and the corresponding boulder leaders. If you'd like to join a Boulder please reach out to the leader via email.
1 MAXIMIZE REIMBURSEMENT - Jim Heatherman
2  RECRUITING - Reid Williams
3  ONBOARDING - Michael Allison
4  RETENTION - Aaron Holman
5  COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT - Krista Shoutko
6  PATIENT EXPERIENCE - Ryan Macan
7  NON-CLINICAL OPERATIONS - Jim Heatherman
8  SPORTS MEDICINE - Matt Dobratz

importantInfo.title: Check your ABNs !
importantInfo.body: Please make sure your ABN charge (97139) is getting the check mark on your checkout screen and is not crossed out when you are sending your note/claim to the billing department. We are seeing an increase in claims going out with the ABN charge, but the ABN charge is crossed out or greyed out on the checkout screen, so the ABN charge doesn't actually get applied to the patient.

importantInfo.title: Marketing Team Needs CE Contacts
importantInfo.body: Krista, Winnie, and Peyton are building community contacts near each clinic. If a patient works somewhere that could benefit from our services, get the HR rep's contact info.

importantInfo.title: Flex Schedule Criteria Perk Updates
importantInfo.body: Providers may now flex their schedule during a PTO week if they maintain a current YTD average of 50+ treatments per week. Refer to the flex schedule manual for the full explanation of the perk.

importantInfo.title: Community Partners
importantInfo.body: Not everyone knows who their community partners are. The full list is on SharePoint. Reach out to any partner you haven't heard from recently and set up a CE event!

importantInfo.title: Case Overview Dashboard / Old Patients
importantInfo.body:
Use the Case Overview Dashboard weekly to go through open cases, reach out to patients who haven't been in, update treatment plans, or discharge inactive cases.

https://support.promptemr.com/article/704-the-active-cases-dashboard-tool

importantInfo.title: PCC / Admin Reminders
importantInfo.body:
1. Dashboard: Review Eligibility, Intake Conflicts, and Benefits Verification
2. Demographics: Gender is required so please ensure it's filled out for all patients
3. Referring Providers: Must be entered on each Case for Plan of Care and Medicare processing

importantInfo.title: POC
importantInfo.body: If a new patient with a referral cancels or no-call no-shows, please email referrals.

importantInfo.title: Provider Daily Rocks
importantInfo.body:
Use your downtime wisely - every provider has 3 daily rocks:

1. Deliver exceptional one-on-one care
2. Build trusting relationships via community engagement
3. Complete billing & documentation in a timely manner

importantInfo.title: Patient Experience Tip
importantInfo.body: *When patients lie prone on your table, they do notice if the underside of your table is dirty. Please make it a habit to wipe off any dust or pick up any trash below your table!*
